race.name: L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Étape Bulgaria by Tour de France - Peshtera
race.tagline: Experience the Tour de France atmosphere on fully closed roads in the Rhodope Mountains

climate.description: Warm summer conditions typical of Bulgarian Rhodope Mountains in early July; expect temperatures 20-30°C with potential afternoon showers. Specific forecasts unavailable.

terrain.surface: Fully closed paved roads through Rhodope Mountains featuring sustained climbs, technical descents, and scenic reservoir views.
features: Fully closed paved roads through Rhodope Mountains featuring sustained climbs, technical descents, and scenic reservoir views

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 79. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 76.

course_description.character: Two laps of a roughly 58 km closed-road circuit through the Western Rhodope Mountains for the long race (112 km / 2,140m), one lap for the short race. Start and finish are in central Peshtera; the circuit climbs to the Sveti Konstantin resort at approximately 1,350m — the course high point — before descending past the Batak Reservoir and through the historic town of Batak back toward Peshtera. Because the long race repeats the lap, the St. Konstantin climb comes twice.
course_description.signature_challenge: The St. Konstantin climb, ridden twice on the 112 km long course — the second ascent arriving with a full lap already in the legs.
suffering_zones: First ascent to Sveti Konstantin resort (1,350m) — the course's high point
Descent through the Batak Gorge area — technical, high-speed, specifically flagged by the organizer
Second ascent to St. Konstantin (long race only) — repeating the climb on tired legs

logistics.transport: Start/finish in Peshtera city center. Fully closed roads. Race Village provides Tour de France atmosphere. International travelers should fly into Sofia (2.5hr drive). Early registration recommended[1].

history.origin_story: Launched January 10, 2023 as inaugural edition. 2026 marks 4th edition (2023-2026). Part of global L'Étape Series across 20+ countries[1][4].

biased_opinion.summary: L'Étape Bulgaria is a genuine A.S.O.-licensed entry in the global L'Étape by Tour de France series, not a locally branded imitation — and it delivers what that franchise promises: fully closed roads, Tour de France-style yellow/green/polka-dot classifications, and real competitive timing. Now in its fourth edition, it moved from Velingrad (its first two editions) to Peshtera starting in 2025, and the 2026 race sold out. The catch is course variety: the 112 km long race is two laps of the same 58 km circuit, not a point-to-point journey, and entry is pricier than you'd expect for a regional event of this length.
strengths: Genuine A.S.O.-licensed L'Étape by Tour de France series event, not a locally branded lookalike
Fully closed roads with real competitive timing and Tour de France-style jersey classifications
2026 edition sold out — real and growing demand for a race only in its fourth year
Technical, high-speed descents and a genuine mountain-pass high point (St. Konstantin, ~1,350m) through the Western Rhodope Mountains
weaknesses: The 112 km long course is two laps of the same 58 km circuit — limited course variety for the distance
Relatively expensive for a regional event of this length: €70-€100 depending on registration timing
Race numbers cannot be mailed or collected on race day — pre-event collection is mandatory
Only road or drop-bar gravel bikes are accepted on the long course; triathlon extensions and disc wheels are prohibited
The event moved location once in four editions (Velingrad for the first two, Peshtera from 2025 on), so course specifics may not be stable long-term
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If the appeal is the official L'Étape brand — closed roads, real competition, Tour de France atmosphere — this delivers it honestly. Just know the 102 km is two laps of one circuit, not a point-to-point tour of the Rhodopes.

final_verdict.one_liner: T2 - Premium Tour de France franchise sportive delivering authentic Gran Fondo experience in spectacular Rhodope Mountains. Excellent for climbers seeking closed-road adventure.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want genuine closed-road racing with official Tour de France branding and don't mind a repeated-lap course. Skip if you want route variety over a full 100+ km distance.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want a point-to-point Rhodope Mountains route instead of a repeated lap: check regional Bulgarian Cycling Federation road races. If you want the L'Étape brand with a different course character: other national L'Étape by Tour de France events.

eligibility.notes: 2026 fourth Bulgarian edition on closed roads in Peshtera with race and ride scheduled for July 5, 2026.; last edition evidenced: 2026; via sonar-pro eligibility_audit
